sections.paragraph:
σκυτάλη (σκῠτά˘λη, ἡ,)

I. a staff, cudgel, club, Anth.

II. at Sparta, a staff or baton, used as a cypher for writing dispatches:—a strip of leather was rolled slantwise round it, on which the dispatches were written lengthwise, so that when unrolled they were unintelligible: commanders abroad had a staff of like thickness, round which they rolled these papers, and so were able to read the dispatches:—hence σκυτάλη came to mean a Spartan dispatch, Thuc., Xen.; and generally a message, Pind.


Etym. deriv. uncertain
